seo.title: Sound Equipment Rentals — BEATROX
seo.description: Beatrox provides professional sound equipment rentals for concerts, events, and activations — including PA systems, monitors, mixing consoles, and on-site audio engineering.

og.title: Sound Equipment Rentals — BEATROX Portland
og.description: Professional PA systems, consoles, and monitoring with experienced engineers — Beatrox sound equipment rentals for concerts and events.

hero.headline: Sound Equipment Rentals
hero.subheadline: Professional PA systems, consoles, and monitoring — with experienced engineers who know how to make them sing.

body.content: Great sound is the foundation of every great event. Beatrox provides professional-grade audio equipment rentals backed by our team of experienced engineers — from intimate brand activations to large-format concert productions. Every rental comes with the option of on-site engineering support.

body.heading: Why Choose Beatrox
items: 15+ years of experience in live event production
Full-service team from design to strike
Trusted by Adidas, Netflix, CNN, Amazon Music

body.heading: Our Process
items: 1. Discovery Call — We learn your vision, venue, and budget
2. Design & Pre-Visualization — See your event before it happens
3. Equipment & Crew — We source everything and assign the right team
4. On-Site Execution — Professional setup, operation, and strike
5. Post-Event Review — We ensure everything exceeded expectations

body.heading: Equipment & Engineering
body.content: Our sound rental inventory includes line array PA systems, digital mixing consoles, stage monitor systems, wireless microphone systems, and in-ear monitor setups. Our Audio Engineer Joshua Vallejos brings 15+ years of touring and live sound experience to every project, ensuring your audio is impeccable from soundcheck to last note.

body.heading: Venue Size Matching
body.content: Choosing the right PA system depends on your venue size and audience count. For rooms up to 200 people, point-source speakers deliver clear, focused sound. For 200–1,000 capacity venues, a small line array provides even coverage and consistent SPL across the audience. For large festivals and arenas, full-scale line arrays with sub arrays and delay towers ensure every seat gets the same powerful experience. We assess your venue, model coverage, and spec the perfect system.

body.heading: Engineer Qualifications & Wireless Frequency Coordination
body.content: Our engineering team includes certified A1 and A2 engineers with touring experience on major festivals and concert tours. For wireless microphone and IEM systems, we perform professional frequency coordination — scanning the RF environment, assigning clear frequencies, and monitoring for interference throughout your event. This is critical in urban venues and festival environments where RF congestion can cause dropouts. We ensure your wireless is rock-solid.

body.heading: Common Equipment
items: L-Acoustics, d&b audiotechnik, and Meyer Sound systems
DiGiCo, Yamaha, and Midas digital consoles
Shure, Sennheiser, and Lectrosonics wireless systems
Scalable from 100-person rooms to 5,000+ capacity venues

items.question: How far in advance should I book?
items.answer: We recommend booking at least 4–6 weeks in advance for standard rentals and 8–12 weeks for custom productions. Last-minute requests are often accommodated depending on availability.

items.question: Do you travel outside Portland?
items.answer: Absolutely. While we're headquartered in Portland, OR, we regularly serve clients across the West Coast and nationwide for touring and large-scale events.

items.question: What's included in the rental?
items.answer: Every rental includes delivery, setup, on-site technical support, and strike. Custom packages can also include content design, pre-visualization, and dedicated project management.

items.question: Do you provide an audio engineer?
items.answer: Yes. We offer experienced A1 (Front of House) and A2 (Monitor) engineers with touring and festival experience. Engineers can be added to any rental package.

items.question: Can you handle wireless microphones in a crowded RF environment?
items.answer: Absolutely. Our team performs professional RF coordination, scanning the venue, assigning clear frequencies, and monitoring for interference — ensuring reliable wireless performance even in congested urban or festival environments.
